{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Advisor III - Offshore Rotation",
  "description": "Habrams Energy offers you engineering, procurement, construction management, operations, and maintenance services for oil &amp; gas projects in the Gulf of Guinea. We provide specialist solutions to international oil companies, marginal field operators, and service companies.

Our work ranges from retrofitting and de-bottlenecking to designing complete facilities &acirc;&euro;&ldquo; with seamless transition from construction to operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Logistics Advisor III - Offshore Rotation

Job Location: Lagos

Main Functions


	Provide day-to-day on-site logistics oversight for offshore operations, ensuring material and personnel management are executed efficiently and accurately.
	Support Wells Operations Supervisors and collaborate with MODU contractors and service partners.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee delivery, condition, and storage of rig and third-party equipment.
	Maintain accurate material and personnel manifests.
	Conduct internal assessments and audits for inventory control compliance.
	Work 12+ hour shifts on a 28 days on / 28 days off rotation.
	Collaborate effectively in multicultural, multidisciplinary teams.


Skills &amp; Qualifications


	Experience in supply chain and logistics, preferably international operations.
	Strong English communication skills.
	Excellent interpersonal and relationship-building skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their&nbsp;updated Resume&nbsp;to:&nbsp;hesprojectrecruitment@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Logistics Advisor III - Offshore Rotation Job at Habrams Energy




        
    

        
        Habrams Energy offers you engineering, procurement, construction management, operations, and maintenance services for oil & gas projects in the Gulf of Guinea. We provide specialist solutions to international oil companies, marginal field operators, and service companies.

Our work ranges from retrofitting and de-bottlenecking to designing complete facilities â€“ with seamless transition from construction to operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Advisor III - Offshore Rotation

Job Location: Lagos

Main Functions
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provide day-to-day on-site logistics oversight for offshore operations, ensuring material and personnel management are executed efficiently and accurately.
	Support Wells Operations Supervisors and collaborate with MODU contractors and service partners.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee delivery, condition, and storage of rig and third-party equipment.
	Maintain accurate material and personnel manifests.
	Conduct internal assessments and audits for inventory control compliance.
	Work 12+ hour shifts on a 28 days on / 28 days off rotation.
	Collaborate effectively in multicultural, multidisciplinary teams.


Skills & Qualifications


	Experience in supply chain and logistics, preferably international operations.
	Strong English communication skills.
	Excellent interpersonal and relationship-building skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Recruitment Consultant & HR Support Job at Talenture Group
content:
Talenture Group - We specialize in Financial Services, Technology and Telecoms recruitment, delivering innovative solutions to clients. With a focus on expanding our dynamic sales team, we aim to drive growth within the commercial banking sector as a leading provider of cutting-edge workforce solution.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Recruitment Consultant & HR Support

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Are you a sales-driven graduate with little or no experience in a sales-focused environment but eager to learn, grow, and explore exciting opportunities within a consulting firm? Are you motivated to build a successful career in a target-driven environment?
	We are looking for a Graduate Sales and Recruitment Consultant to join our dynamic team. This role offers a unique opportunity to work with leading multinational and blue-chip organizations, supporting them with talent advisory, recruitment, and HR solutions.
	Successful candidates will receive structured training and onboarding, providing a strong foundation for a long-term career in business development, recruitment consulting, and executive search.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the end-to-end recruitment process, from role briefing to candidate placement
	Source, screen and shortlist qualified candidates using various recruitment channels
	Conduct candidate interviews and assessments
	Build and maintain strong candidate pipelines and talent pools
	Coordinate interview schedules for candidates
	Provide high-quality candidate delivery within agreed timelines
	Provide HR support duties with HR administrative processes and documentation
	Assist with onboarding and induction coordination for new hires
	Maintain accurate employee and candidate records
	Support HR with policy implementation, compliance, training and internal HR processes
	Assist with preparation of HR reports and recruitment metrics


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Human Resources or social science related discipline
	Minimum of 1 year experience as a Recruitment Consultant, preferably within a recruitment agency
	Proven ability to source and deliver quality candidates
	Good understanding of HR processes and HR administrative support
	Strong communication, organization and interpersonal skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment
	Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks
	If you are passionate about recruitment, talent sourcing and HR operations, this is an excellent opportunity to grow your career within a dynamic and professional environment.


Note


	This is an urgent role and only candidates within commutable distance to Lekki Phase 1 should apply.
	Qualified candidates will be considered immediately.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head Of Operations",
  "description": "Femaus Consults is a dynamic brand dedicated to driving excellence across diverse sectors. We specialize in capacity building, recruitment tailored to elevate businesses and organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head Of Operations

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities&nbsp;


	Develop and implement operational strategies that align with the company&rsquo;s overall goals and objectives.
	Provide leadership and direction to all operational functions, including production, logistics, supply chain, customer service, and facility management.
	Ensure continuous improvement in operations, driving innovation and best practices across the company.
	Analyze current operational processes and workflows to identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements.
	Lead initiatives to improve productivity, reduce operational costs, and enhance quality across all business functions.
	Develop and implement key performance indicators (KPIs) to monitor operational performance and ensure alignment with strategic goals.
	Oversee the company&rsquo;s supply chain operations to ensure timely and cost-effective delivery of products and services.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@femausconsults.ng&nbsp;using &quot;HOPEG&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Head Of Operations Job at Femaus Consults




        
    

        
        Femaus Consults is a dynamic brand dedicated to driving excellence across diverse sectors. We specialize in capacity building, recruitment tailored to elevate businesses and organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head Of Operations

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and implement operational strategies that align with the company’s overall goals and objectives.
	Provide leadership and direction to all operational functions, including production, logistics, supply chain, customer service, and facility management.
	Ensure continuous improvement in operations, driving innovation and best practices across the company.
	Analyze current operational processes and workflows to identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements.
	Lead initiatives to improve productivity, reduce operational costs, and enhance quality across all business functions.
	Develop and implement key performance indicators (KPIs) to monitor operational performance and ensure alignment with strategic goals.
	Oversee the company’s supply chain operations to ensure timely and cost-effective delivery of products and services.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "HOPEG" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Job at Femaus Consults




        
    

        
        Femaus Consults is a dynamic brand dedicated to driving excellence across diverse sectors. We specialize in capacity building, recruitment tailored to elevate businesses and organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Financial Officer (CFO)

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Lead the financial planning and analysis (FP&A) functions, ensuring the company’s financial health and stability.
	Develop and implement the financial strategy to support the company's goals and objectives.
	Ensure compliance with tax regulations, financial audits, and other statutory requirements.
	Monitor the company’s financial performance, highlighting key insights and trends to senior management.
	Identify financial risks and create strategies to mitigate them.
	Establish and monitor internal controls to safeguard the company’s assets and ensure the integrity of financial records.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor's or Master's Degree with 5-10 years of experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "CFOEG" as the subject of the email.

Note: Candidates who fail to comply will not be considered.
